Text: What is an object in Object-Oriented Programming?
Which OOP principle describes hiding internal state and requiring all interaction to be performed through an object's methods?
Which keyword is used to create a new object in C#?
What does 'S' stand for in SOLID principles?
Which keyword allows a class to inherit from a base class in C#?
What is the base class for all types in C#?
Which data type is used to store true or false values?
What is Method Overloading in C#?
Options: Methods with the same name but different parameters

Text: What does the 'static' keyword mean for a class member?
Options: It belongs to the class itself, not instances

Text: What is the primary role of the Garbage Collector in .NET?
Options: Free memory by removing unreferenced objects

Text: Can you instantiate an abstract class?
Options: Only if it has no methods

Text: How many base classes can a C# class inherit from?
What exception is thrown when accessing a member on a null object?
What is a constructor?
Options: A method to delete an object
A special method called when an object is created

Text: What is the difference between 'virtual' and 'abstract' methods?
Options: Virtual has a body, abstract has no body

Text: What happens during 'Boxing' in .NET?
Options: Converting a Value Type into a Reference Type

Text: Where are Value Types (like structs and ints) typically allocated in memory?
What is the difference between 'ref' and 'out' parameters?
Options: Ref requires initialization before passing, out does not

Text: Which access modifier makes a member accessible only within its own class and derived classes?
What is the difference between 'const' and 'readonly' fields?
Options: Readonly can be assigned at runtime in a constructor

Text: What keyword is used to handle exceptions in C#?
Which interface must be implemented to allow an object to be iterated over using a 'foreach' loop?
What was ALLOWED in an abstract class but FORBIDDEN in an interface (prior to C# 8)?
What does the 'where T : new()' constraint mean in generic programming?
Options: T must have a parameterless constructor
T must be a value type

Text: What is the purpose of the 'lock' statement in C#?
Options: Ensure thread safety by preventing simultaneous access

Text: What is a 'Delegate' in C#?
Options: A type that represents references to methods

Text: Which interface is used to manually release unmanaged resources?
How does 'await' work in an async method?
Options: Suspends evaluation of the method and yields control back to the caller

Text: What does the 'yield return' statement do under the hood?
Options: Generates a custom state machine for lazy evaluation

Text: What is Contravariance in C# generics?
Options: Using a more derived type than specified
Using a less derived (more generic) type than specified

Text: What is 'Reflection' in .NET?
Options: The ability of a program to inspect its own metadata at runtime

Text: What is the Large Object Heap (LOH) in .NET?
Options: A memory segment for objects larger than 85,000 bytes

Text: What does the 'volatile' keyword ensure?
Options: The variable will never be optimized by the compiler into a register

Text: When should you use ValueTask instead of Task?
Options: When the result of an async operation might already be available synchronously

Text: What does 'stackalloc' do?
Options: Allocates a block of memory on the stack instead of the heap
Allocates memory on the hard drive
